changed_functions.name: NsiGetParameterEx
key_changes: Added a gate on the WIL feature flag 'Feature_TCPIP_2025_2511_SFI_58579577_Fix' at 0x14002A367. When the feature is enabled AND the provider capability bit [provider+0x10] & 2 is set AND the computed size esi >= 0x100000, the function returns STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER (0xC000000D) early. When the feature is disabled or the bit is clear, control transfers to the original grow-and-retry loop at loc_14002A3C9 unchanged. This is a feature-gated staged rollout with the old path retained; default behavior equals the unpatched build.
Register reallocation: the allocated-buffer pointer moved from r14 (unpatched) to r15 (patched); the provider pointer moved from r15 to r14. No behavioral effect.
One allocation site converted from ExAllocatePool3 to NsipAllocateLowPriority, matching the other allocation paths in the function.

The error/success separation in the grow-and-retry loop is structurally identical in both builds. Error paths (allocation failure, negative callback status, post-loop negative status) route to the WPP error-logging block then to the shared free/exit tail; the memmove is reached only on success in both builds.
changed_functions.security_notes: No delivered security-relevant change. The report premise that an error-logging block falls through unconditionally to the copy is not present in either build. In the unpatched build the memmove at 0x14002A477 is at a lower address than the error-logging block at 0x14002A4B8; the memmove is reached only on the success paths (0x14002A419 jnb loc_14002A469, or the post-loop non-negative-status fall-through) and is immediately followed by 'jmp short loc_14002A4F0' at 0x14002A47C. The error-logging block at 0x14002A4B8 ends with WPP_SF_D at 0x14002A4EB and continues into the shared free/exit tail at 0x14002A4F0 (test r14 -> conditional ExFreePoolWithTag -> jmp loc_14002A103), never reaching the copy. The patched build preserves this exact structure (copy at 0x14002A4DC, error block at 0x14002A51D, shared tail at 0x14002A555, exit at 0x14002A0FC). The only functional edit is the feature-gated early-out described in key_changes, which is disabled by default and grants no attacker primitive.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED critical section (NsiGetParameterEx @ 0x140029E30):
0x14002A35F : cmp     esi, 100000h            ; computed size vs 1MB
0x14002A365 : jnb     loc_14002A465           ; -> post-loop check
0x14002A3B0 : call    __imp_ExAllocatePool3
0x14002A3BC : mov     r14, rax                ; P = allocation
0x14002A3C2 : jz      loc_14002A47E           ; alloc fail -> logging block
0x14002A3F9 : call    _guard_dispatch_icall   ; provider callback
0x14002A400 : cmp     eax, 0C0000023h         ; buffer-too-small?
0x14002A405 : jz      short loc_14002A41B     ; retry (double size)
0x14002A409 : js      loc_14002A4B8           ; error -> err_log block
0x14002A419 : jnb     short loc_14002A469     ; success -> copy setup
0x14002A465 : test    ebx, ebx
0x14002A467 : js      short loc_14002A4B8     ; error -> err_log block
0x14002A470 : add     rdx, r14                ; src = P + offset (success only)
0x14002A477 : call    memmove
0x14002A47C : jmp     short loc_14002A4F0     ; -> shared cleanup
0x14002A4B3 : mov     ebx, 0C000009Ah         ; STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES
0x14002A4B8 : [err_log block] ... 0x14002A4EB call WPP_SF_D  ; reached AFTER copy; does NOT reach it
0x14002A4F0 : test    r14, r14                ; shared cleanup
0x14002A50A : jmp     loc_14002A103           ; return status

PATCHED critical section (NsiGetParameterEx @ 0x140029E30):
0x14002A367 : mov     eax, cs:Feature_TCPIP_2025_2511_SFI_58579577_Fix__private_featureState
0x14002A376 : call    Feature_TCPIP_2025_2511_SFI_58579577_Fix__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline
0x14002A37D : jz      short loc_14002A3C9     ; feature OFF -> original loop (unchanged)
0x14002A37F : test    byte ptr [r14+10h], 2
0x14002A384 : jz      short loc_14002A3C9     ; capability bit clear -> original loop
0x14002A386 : cmp     esi, 100000h
0x14002A38C : jb      short loc_14002A3D5     ; size < 1MB -> enter loop normally
0x14002A38E : mov     ebx, 0C000000Dh         ; else STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER, log and return
0x14002A4D5 : add     rdx, r15                ; src = P + offset (P now r15, success only)
0x14002A4DC : call    memmove
0x14002A4E1 : jmp     short loc_14002A555     ; -> shared cleanup
0x14002A51D : [err_log block] ... 0x14002A550 call WPP_SF_D
0x14002A555 : test    r15, r15                ; shared cleanup
0x14002A56F : jmp     loc_14002A0FC           ; return status

security_findings.entry_point: NSI parameter-retrieval interface reachable from user mode
call_chain: on success: memmove at 0x14002A477; on error: WPP error-logging block at 0x14002A4B8 -> shared free/exit tail at 0x14002A4F0 -> return
security_findings.description: The report premise (an error-logging block that falls through unconditionally to memcpy, causing a NULL-pointer dereference or kernel information disclosure) is not present in either build. In the unpatched NsiGetParameterEx the memmove at 0x14002A477 sits at a lower address than the error-logging block at 0x14002A4B8 and is followed by 'jmp short loc_14002A4F0' at 0x14002A47C. The memmove is reached only on success (0x14002A419 jnb loc_14002A469, or the post-loop non-negative-status fall-through at 0x14002A465). Every error path (allocation failure 0x14002A3C2, negative callback status 0x14002A409, post-loop negative status 0x14002A467) routes to the error-logging block at 0x14002A4B8, which ends with WPP_SF_D at 0x14002A4EB and continues into the shared free/exit tail at 0x14002A4F0 (test r14 -> conditional ExFreePoolWithTag -> jmp loc_14002A103), never reaching the copy. The patched build preserves this exact structure. The only functional edit is a feature-gated early-out (Feature_TCPIP_2025_2511_SFI_58579577_Fix at 0x14002A367) that returns STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER for size >= 0x100000 requests to providers with capability bit [provider+0x10] & 2, only when the feature is enabled; the original code path is retained in the else branch (loc_14002A3C9), so default behavior equals the unpatched build.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: No security-relevant primitive is reachable. The feature-gated early-out changes behavior only when Feature_TCPIP_2025_2511_SFI_58579577_Fix is enabled, and even then only converts oversized (>= 1MB) requests to capability-flagged providers into a STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER return.
security_findings.evidence:
UNPATCHED NsiGetParameterEx (error/success paths are mutually exclusive):
0x14002A409 : js      loc_14002A4B8           ; negative callback status -> err_log
0x14002A419 : jnb     short loc_14002A469     ; success -> copy setup
0x14002A465 : test    ebx, ebx
0x14002A467 : js      short loc_14002A4B8     ; negative status -> err_log
0x14002A469 : mov     edx, [rdi+4Ch]          ; offset (copy setup, success only)
0x14002A470 : add     rdx, r14                ; src = P + offset
0x14002A473 : mov     rcx, [rdi+40h]          ; dst = output buffer
0x14002A477 : call    memmove
0x14002A47C : jmp     short loc_14002A4F0     ; -> shared cleanup
0x14002A4B8 : [error-logging block]
0x14002A4EB : call    WPP_SF_D
0x14002A4F0 : test    r14, r14                ; shared cleanup (reached from BOTH copy and err_log)
0x14002A4F3 : jz      loc_14002A103
0x14002A4FE : call    __imp_ExFreePoolWithTag
0x14002A50A : jmp     loc_14002A103

PATCHED NsiGetParameterEx (feature gate added, structure otherwise identical):
0x14002A367 : mov     eax, cs:Feature_TCPIP_2025_2511_SFI_58579577_Fix__private_featureState
0x14002A37D : jz      short loc_14002A3C9     ; feature OFF -> original loop
0x14002A384 : jz      short loc_14002A3C9     ; capability bit clear -> original loop
0x14002A38E : mov     ebx, 0C000000Dh         ; STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER (gated early-out)
0x14002A4DC : call    memmove                 ; success only
0x14002A51D : [error-logging block] -> 0x14002A555 shared cleanup -> 0x14002A0FC return
security_findings.assembly: See changed_functions[0].assembly_diff for the full unpatched and patched critical sections of NsiGetParameterEx @ 0x140029E30.
security_findings.notes: A content-based function diff (matching across relocations, ignoring addresses and operands) confirms NsiGetParameterEx is the only changed function. Two WIL feature-staging helpers were added in the patched build (Feature_TCPIP_2025_2511_SFI_58579577_Fix__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline and __private_IsEnabledFallback). The 'Fix'/'SFI' naming denotes a tracked servicing item staged behind a WIL feature flag; the delivered default path is unchanged from the unpatched build.

summary: No delivered security-relevant change in netio.sys between these builds. The only changed function is NsiGetParameterEx (0x140029E30). Its sole functional edit is a feature-gated early-out: when Feature_TCPIP_2025_2511_SFI_58579577_Fix is enabled and the provider advertises capability bit [provider+0x10] & 2 and the computed size is >= 0x100000, the function returns STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER (0xC000000D); otherwise control falls to the original grow-and-retry loop unchanged. The reported error-path fall-through to memcpy (claimed NULL-pointer dereference / kernel information disclosure) does not exist in either build: the error-logging block at 0x14002A4B8 continues to the shared free/exit tail at 0x14002A4F0, and the memmove at 0x14002A477 (lower address, success paths only) jumps to that same tail. Remaining differences are register reallocation and one ExAllocatePool3 -> NsipAllocateLowPriority conversion. Two WIL feature-staging helper functions were added to support the flag.
